jokes.text: Frank wearing skinny jeans, described as looking ridiculous and having a 'Humpty Dumpty vibe'
jokes.setup: Frank tries to look hip by wearing skinny jeans
jokes.punchline: The visual of an older, out-of-shape man in tight jeans creates a ridiculous appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Frank's delusional self-image creates natural visual comedy

jokes.text: I am getting a tremendous amount of enjoyment watching you parade around in those things. So I say keep them.
jokes.setup: Frank asks what they think of his skinny jeans
jokes.punchline: Dennis admits he enjoys watching Frank embarrass himself
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Dennis's narcissistic enjoyment of others' humiliation with elegant phrasing

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I am getting a tremendous amount of enjoyment watching you parade around in those things. So I say keep them.

jokes.text: Big news, bitches! The ban's been lifted. And we are back in. Flipadelphia, here we come!
jokes.setup: Mac bursts in with dramatic news
jokes.punchline: The 'big news' is about a drinking game tournament with a silly portmanteau name
jokes.explanation: The wordplay 'Flipadelphia' is clever and quotable, delivered with perfect overenthusiasm

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Big news, bitches! The ban's been lifted. And we are back in. Flipadelphia, here we come!

jokes.text: Flip, flip, Flipadelphia! chanting sequence
jokes.setup: The gang gets excited about the tournament
jokes.punchline: Their chant becomes increasingly ridiculous and overly enthusiastic
jokes.explanation: Establishes the running gag while showing the gang's childish enthusiasm

dialogue.line: Flip, flip, Flipadelphia!

jokes.text: No, we did poison the shit out of them. But they didn't have any proof.
jokes.setup: Frank asks if they were framed for poisoning
jokes.punchline: Mac casually admits they actually did poison people
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy - the casual admission of poisoning people over a drinking game is shockingly funny

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No, we did poison the shit out of them. But they didn't have any proof.

jokes.text: This year we're gonna jam it right up their butt holes... Yeah, me, Dennis and Charlie will jam their butt holes.
jokes.setup: Mac talks about beating their rivals
jokes.punchline: The repeated use of 'butt holes' becomes increasingly ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Juvenile humor that works through repetition but lacks sophistication

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: This year we're gonna jam it right up their butt holes...

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yeah, me, Dennis and Charlie will jam their butt holes.

jokes.text: You're not gonna come anywhere near their butt holes. Let us handle the butt holes.
jokes.setup: Continuation of the butt holes conversation
jokes.punchline: Dennis seriously discussing 'butt hole' strategy
jokes.explanation: Continues the juvenile joke but without adding much new comedic value

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You're not gonna come anywhere near their butt holes. Let us handle the butt holes.

jokes.text: I'm your 'Adelphia.'
jokes.setup: Dee wants to be included on the team
jokes.punchline: She awkwardly tries to make herself part of the 'Flipadelphia' wordplay
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that captures Dee's desperate need to be included

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I'm your 'Adelphia.'

jokes.text: You choked under pressure in '98. You blew the whole thing. We were gonna win. And then you couldn't flip your damn cup over.
jokes.setup: Discussion of why Dee can't be on the team
jokes.punchline: Charlie's specific memory of Dee's past failure
jokes.explanation: Establishes character history while showing the gang's pettiness over a drinking game

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You choked under pressure in '98. You blew the whole thing. We were gonna win. And then you couldn't flip your damn cup over.

jokes.text: Flip cup. Ten years ago? We poisoned you, dude. Remember?
jokes.setup: Art Sloan doesn't recognize them
jokes.punchline: Mac casually mentions poisoning him as a memory refresher
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly casual delivery of something horrific - perfect dark comedy

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Flip cup. Ten years ago? We poisoned you, dude. Remember?

jokes.text: Who's to say we didn't put that very same poison in the drinking water?... He's lying. He doesn't have any poison... No, I don't have any on me. But I do keep some in my fridge at home in the relish jar.
jokes.setup: Mac threatens they might have poisoned the water
jokes.punchline: He admits he's lying but then reveals he actually does keep poison at home
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - admitting the lie while revealing an even more disturbing truth

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Who's to say we didn't put that very same poison in the drinking water?...

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: He's lying. He doesn't have any poison...

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No, I don't have any on me. But I do keep some in my fridge at home in the relish jar.

jokes.text: There's poison in that jar? I thought I was allergic to pickles.
jokes.setup: Mac mentions poison in the relish jar
jokes.punchline: Frank realizes he's been eating poison thinking it was an allergy
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment that's perfectly in character for Frank's obliviousness

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: There's poison in that jar? I thought I was allergic to pickles.

jokes.text: What's in the jar with the skull and crossbones? Oh, that's mayonnaise. That's a decoy. And the mayo? That's shampoo.
jokes.setup: Frank asks about various jars
jokes.punchline: The labeling system is completely backwards and nonsensical
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic that builds perfectly - each revelation is more ridiculous

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: What's in the jar with the skull and crossbones?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Oh, that's mayonnaise. That's a decoy. And the mayo? That's shampoo.

jokes.text: You're telling me I've been putting shampoo on my sandwiches?
jokes.setup: Frank learns the mayo is actually shampoo
jokes.punchline: Frank's delayed realization of what he's been eating
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the absurd jar situation, Frank's confusion is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: You're telling me I've been putting shampoo on my sandwiches?

jokes.text: We came to tell you we're reigniting the rivalry, dick worm.
jokes.setup: Mac explains why they're there
jokes.punchline: The childish insult 'dick worm' delivered seriously
jokes.explanation: Juvenile but effective insult that fits Mac's immature character

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We came to tell you we're reigniting the rivalry, dick worm.

jokes.text: Mac urinating on Art's bar after being called childish
jokes.setup: Art calls Mac childish
jokes.punchline: Mac responds by literally acting like a child and peeing on the bar
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic response - proves Art's point by being even more childish than accused

dialogue.line: Mac urinating on Art's bar after being called childish

jokes.text: Your mother had to have a C-section because of those hands.
jokes.setup: Mac insults Dee's large hands
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific and dark birth-related insult
jokes.explanation: Creatively cruel insult that's both absurd and memorably specific

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Your mother had to have a C-section because of those hands.

jokes.text: Maybe you could be, um, team instigator... I'll instigate.
jokes.setup: Frank needs a role since he's not on the team
jokes.punchline: Frank eagerly accepts the made-up role of 'instigator'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Frank's eagerness to be included in chaos

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Maybe you could be, um, team instigator...

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I'll instigate.

jokes.text: That was, like, 15 years ago. I don't think you'd have any clout there... It doesn't matter, man. I was the king of that place. I'm a legend over there.
jokes.setup: Charlie questions Dennis's fraternity connections
jokes.punchline: Dennis's delusional confidence in his lasting legendary status
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis narcissism - believing he's still relevant after 15 years

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: That was, like, 15 years ago. I don't think you'd have any clout there...

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It doesn't matter, man. I was the king of that place. I'm a legend over there.

jokes.text: I chug dick? No. What? I never- I don't chug dick. That's crazy.
jokes.setup: Dennis sees his photo in the fraternity alumni section
jokes.punchline: Someone has written 'I chug dick' under his photo
jokes.explanation: Dennis's horror at the defacement of his image undermines his 'legend' status

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I chug dick? No. What? I never- I don't chug dick. That's crazy.

jokes.text: Dude, we were cruising in sixth grade in '98, man.
jokes.setup: Dennis says he's from the class of '98
jokes.punchline: The student points out how old Dennis really is
jokes.explanation: Sharp putdown that deflates Dennis's attempt to seem relevant

dialogue.speaker: Frat student
dialogue.line: Dude, we were cruising in sixth grade in '98, man.

jokes.text: Oh, he's a pledge. And we're zapping his nuts with a stun gun every couple of hours.
jokes.setup: Dennis asks about a guy on the floor
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of ongoing torture as hazing
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly, showing how casually cruel the students are

dialogue.line: Oh, he's a pledge. And we're zapping his nuts with a stun gun every couple of hours.

jokes.text: Oh, man! You got any ludes? Ludes? I don't think they make those anymore, man.
jokes.setup: Frank asks the students for drugs
jokes.punchline: Frank asks for quaaludes, which are decades out of date
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Frank's outdated drug knowledge

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Oh, man! You got any ludes?

dialogue.speaker: Frat student
dialogue.line: Ludes? I don't think they make those anymore, man.

jokes.text: Don't do that shit again, you little idiot! Have some respect for a brother, okay? I'm a legend around this place.
jokes.setup: Dennis gets stunned again
jokes.punchline: His pathetic attempt to assert authority while clearly being dominated
jokes.explanation: Dennis's delusional self-importance contrasted with his powerless position

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Don't do that shit again, you little idiot! Have some respect for a brother, okay? I'm a legend around this place.

jokes.text: Flip cup? What are you, a freshman? That is so lame.
jokes.setup: Dennis tries to recruit them for flip cup
jokes.punchline: They dismiss flip cup as childish despite their own behavior
jokes.explanation: Good irony - calling flip cup lame while torturing pledges

dialogue.speaker: Frat student
dialogue.line: Flip cup? What are you, a freshman? That is so lame.

jokes.text: Dennis and Frank's drug-fueled rant about the disrespectful frat kids
jokes.setup: They return from the fraternity house defeated
jokes.punchline: Their increasingly unhinged rant while clearly high on Adderall
jokes.explanation: Good escalating rant that shows their drug-addled state and wounded pride

dialogue.line: Dennis and Frank's drug-fueled rant about the disrespectful frat kids

jokes.text: Yeah, in that movie, Matt Damon played a genius janitor. You're just a janitor.
jokes.setup: Charlie explains his Good Will Hunting plan
jokes.punchline: Dennis points out the fatal flaw in Charlie's logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery that completely deflates Charlie's plan with brutal efficiency

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah, in that movie, Matt Damon played a genius janitor. You're just a janitor.

jokes.text: Right. You stumped me with that one. That's a good point.
jokes.setup: Dennis points out Charlie isn't a genius
jokes.punchline: Charlie immediately admits defeat without understanding the implication
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie obliviousness - agreeing with the insult without realizing it

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Right. You stumped me with that one. That's a good point.

jokes.punchline: The sexual assault described as casual hazing
jokes.explanation: Pushes dark comedy too far without sufficient setup or character justification

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Or I'd take a banana and stick it up some guy's ass in front of his best friend. And I'd be like, 'Hey! Banana ass! How you doing?'

jokes.text: Or maybe I'd, like, uh- I'd like take the tip of my penis and stick it in a guy's mouth for, like, just a second, you know what I mean? Like while he was sleeping.
jokes.setup: Frank continues describing his 'pranks'
jokes.punchline: He describes sexual assault as a harmless prank

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Or maybe I'd, like, uh- I'd like take the tip of my penis and stick it in a guy's mouth for, like, just a second, you know what I mean? Like while he was sleeping.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Well, what I'm doing is I'm cutting the shower curtain in half waist high so that when he's in using it, people can see his junk.

jokes.text: When we shower together and wash each other off.
jokes.setup: Dennis and Charlie think the paint party is over
jokes.punchline: The girl reveals the 'better part' involves group showering

dialogue.speaker: College girl
dialogue.line: When we shower together and wash each other off.

jokes.text: Oh! Bet you're reading a lot of Gordon Wood, huh! You read your Gordon Wood and you regurgitate it from a textbook and you think you're wicked awesome doing that!

jokes.punchline: He recites the movie dialogue completely out of context

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh! Bet you're reading a lot of Gordon Wood, huh! You read your Gordon Wood and you regurgitate it from a textbook and you think you're wicked awesome doing that!

jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that deflates Charlie's attempt at intellectual superiority

dialogue.speaker: Frat student
dialogue.line: I have no idea what you're talking about. You are an idiot.

jokes.text: Goddamn it. This feels forced. I... I'm not into it. I'm definitely forcing it.
jokes.setup: The gang tries to get excited about their rivalry
jokes.punchline: Dennis breaks character to admit the forced nature of their enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment where Dennis acknowledges the artificial nature of their manufactured conflict

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Goddamn it. This feels forced. I... I'm not into it. I'm definitely forcing it.

dialogue.line: I thought you guys believed in me. You were so proud of me...

dialogue.line: Oh, no. We knew you were gonna choke.

jokes.text: Oh, Flipadelphia! Oh, sh... Was that today?

dialogue.line: Oh, Flipadelphia! Oh, sh... Was that today?

jokes.text: Oh, Flipadelphia! Oh, sh... Was that today? Yeah, dude.

jokes.punchline: Gang realizes they completely forgot about it

dialogue.line: Oh, Flipadelphia! Oh, sh... Was that today? Yeah, dude.

jokes.text: I am a legend.
jokes.setup: Art slams the door on them after calling them savages
jokes.punchline: Dennis still insists he's a legend despite all evidence to the contrary
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending for Dennis's delusion arc - still believing his own hype after total failure

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I am a legend.

jokes.text: Who slams a door? Babies. That guy's got some real growing up to do. I mean, have some respect, for Christ's sake. Goddamn savage. I am a legend.

jokes.punchline: Dennis calling Art immature while being completely oblivious to their own behavior

dialogue.line: Who slams a door? Babies. That guy's got some real growing up to do. I mean, have some respect, for Christ's sake. Goddamn savage. I am a legend.
